name: Lifting Embodied World Models for Planning and Control

description: arXiv:2604.26182v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: World models of embodied agents predict future observations conditioned on an action taken by the agent. For complex embodiments, action spaces are high-dimensional and difficult to specify: for example, precisely controlling a human agent requires specifying the motion of each joint. This makes the world model hard to control and expens...
author: Alex N. Wang, Trevor Darrell, Pavel Izmailov, Yutong Bai, Amir Bar
